title: pastinaca minima
intro: Pastinaca minima, also known as the lesser or wild parsnip, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of furanocoumarins, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pastinaca minima include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic, making it useful for treating issues such as indigestion, bloating, and urinary problems. Herbal preparations of Pastinaca minima can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
